column4: Work Performed
Project kick-off; went through the requirements and mapped the candidate verification flow end to end
Finalised the architecture: async FastAPI backend, Next.js frontend, PostgreSQL, with the AI work split into separate single-purpose agents
Designed the Postgres schema: a staging table for raw parsed data and a main table for verified candidates, plus interviews, verifications and a skill taxonomy
Set up the repo, the Python virtualenv, Alembic and the local Postgres, and the base service structure
Built the FastAPI application factory, the pydantic settings layer and the async SQLAlchemy engine and session
Added the SQLAlchemy models for candidates_staging and candidates_main with UUID primary keys and JSONB columns for the parsed profile and analysis
Added the interview, interview_question and verification models with their foreign keys back to the candidate
Added the skill_taxonomy, uploaded_document and background_task models
Set up Alembic and wrote the initial migration, including the pg_trgm extension for fuzzy name search
Built the candidate repository: create and fetch staging, and the staging to main promotion logic
Built the candidate registration endpoint and service that creates the staging row and generates the candidate ref
Set up the async background-task handling with a tasks table that the frontend polls for status
Wired up the OpenAI client wrapper and the base agent structure with the prompt files
Scaffolded the Next.js app-router frontend and the routing
Added the shared shadcn UI components and the ky-based API client
Built the onboarding chat shell and the Zustand stores for the chat and onboarding state
Worked on the Agent 1 resume-parsing prompt to extract structured fields
Got Agent 1 parsing resumes into a structured profile with skills, experience and education
Added vision parsing so scanned and image PDFs go through the vision model instead of plain text extraction
Normalised the parsed skills against the skill taxonomy so search and matching stay consistent
Added the resume scoring with sub-scores and the analytics it pulls out
Wrote the Agent 1 background handler that saves the parsed profile and analysis onto the staging row
Built the resume-review card so the candidate confirms the parsed profile before continuing
Started Agent 2 (duplicate detection) using a pg_trgm fuzzy match on name plus email and phone to shortlist candidates
Finished Agent 2 with the LLM comparison over the shortlist, returning unique, duplicate or uncertain
Added the promotion from staging into the main talent pool once a candidate clears the duplicate check
Started Agent 3 (ID verification), sending the document image through the vision model
Handled Aadhaar, PAN and Passport with the mandatory-document ordering
Added the name and DOB match against the claimed details and the document authenticity confidence
Built the verification records and the per-document status flow
Built the ID upload widget with the step-by-step mandatory-document prompts
Worked on the Agent 4 prompt to generate interview questions from the candidate profile
Got Agent 4 generating questions targeted at the candidate's actual skills with expected-answer points
Worked on the Agent 5 scoring prompt and the evaluation rubric
Finished Agent 5 scoring the transcript into an overall score, the L1 pass/fail and a recommendation
Wired the interview question and answer loop into the chatbot
Built the interview recording and transcription UI with the video widget
Added the scoring task polling and cached the result on the candidate so it loads instantly
Showed the readiness score, L1 status and recommendation on the result screen
Added the re-interview controls so an admin can grant another attempt
Reworked the onboarding chatbot into a proper state machine, one step at a time
Made the chatbot sessions resumable by persisting the onboarding state to localStorage
Added the email lookup and restore so returning candidates pick up exactly where they left off
Cleaned up the typed chat message rendering and the flow handling
Got the new requirement set from the client, went through it and planned the changes
Added the candidate fields they asked for (current CTC, expected CTC, notice period, working status) with the migration
Added current and preferred location, and made Aadhaar and PAN mandatory in the ID verification step
Added the resume score and the general analytics from the resume (tenure, employment gaps, company-type mix)
Captured the Git and LinkedIn URLs and added the LinkedIn vs resume cross check via the Coresignal lookup
Added the service vs product company classification, the value-addition check, certifications and the resume pros and cons
Stored the interview video link and the full interview context, and made sure everything persists to the DB
Added LMS course recommendations for profiles that come from the LMS, and set up Zoho for project tracking
Built the hiring manager dashboard with the pool stats, domain breakdown and recent candidates
Built the JD search with the weighted mandatory/preferred skill-match ranking and the candidate detail page
Wrapped up the candidate data-model changes and ran the migrations
Interview as a Service: Set up the HeyGen LiveAvatar account and got the streaming API key
Interview as a Service: Did a basic LiveAvatar demo, got the avatar WebRTC stream rendering in the browser
Tidied up the hiring manager search, added pagination and the filters
Interview as a Service: Got the avatar to speak by sending text through the LiveAvatar session repeat call
Interview as a Service: Fixed the avatar going silent; it was calling repeat before the session reached the connected state, so I fixed the event timing
Interview as a Service: Hooked up Deepgram nova-2 streaming speech to text over a websocket for the candidate audio
Interview as a Service: Got the realtime transcript working with the avatar, handling the interim and final results
Interview as a Service: Wired the basic question and follow-up loop, sending the transcript to the model and the reply back to the avatar
Got LMS access from the team and went through how SmartRecruitz would plug into it
Started the LMS integration: built the recruitment module and the candidate to LMS-user mapping table
Wired the approval flow and the short-lived token handoff between the LMS and SmartRecruitz
Started merging the SmartRecruitz database into the LMS Postgres database
Moved the tables into the LMS application schema with the lms_recruit_ prefix and matched their naming conventions
Pointed the FastAPI backend at the shared LMS database, updated the models, FKs and the Alembic version schema, and verified it still worked
Wired the location filter into the hiring manager search, matching current or preferred location
Started the standalone auth: argon2 password hashing and HS256 JWTs, and set up the Google sign in
Finished the auth: the hiring manager login, the get_current_user dependency and the route guards
Interview as a Service: Tested the LiveAvatar interview demo end to end
Stabilised both apps, sorted the env config and the run scripts, and wrote up the docs
Added the Google sign in for both candidates and hiring managers, with the intent-based OAuth callback assigning the role
